name: Chitragupta
description: Chitragupta is a Hindu deity assigned with the task of maintaining complete records of individual human beings' actions on Earth. Upon a person's death, Chitragupta tallies the good and bad deeds, using these records to determine the person's assignment to heaven or hell, or to rebirth on Earth. He is often depicted holding a pen, inkpot, and a ledger, symbolizing his meticulous record-keeping. He is considered an important deity, especially by the Kayastha community who claim descent from him. Chitragupta is believed to be either an incarnation of Brahma, the creator, or an assistant to Yama, the god of death. His role is crucial in the cosmic order of karma and dharma, ensuring that justice is served in the afterlife. He is worshipped for guidance in living a righteous life and for a favorable judgment after death. Temples dedicated to Chitragupta are found throughout India, where devotees offer prayers for forgiveness of sins and blessings for a virtuous life. His existence emphasizes the importance of ethical behavior and the consequences of one's actions, reinforcing the Hindu concept of karma.
subtitle: Divine Accountant of Deeds and Karma

keyMyths.title: Origin of Chitragupta
keyMyths.description: Brahma, after creating the world, realized the need for someone to keep track of the deeds of humans. He meditated, and Chitragupta emerged from his body, hence his name.

keyMyths.title: Chitragupta's Judgement
keyMyths.description: Upon death, each soul is presented before Chitragupta, who reads out their deeds from his ledger. Based on this, he assigns the soul to heaven, hell, or rebirth.

keyMyths.title: Worship of Chitragupta
keyMyths.description: The Kayastha community, considered descendants of Chitragupta, worship him during the festival of Bhai Dooj. They believe offering prayers ensures a favorable judgement after death.

sources.author: Swami Sivananda
sources.description: Details the role of Yama and the afterlife, indirectly touching upon Chitragupta's responsibilities.
sources.text: Details the role of Yama and the afterlife, indirectly touching upon Chitragupta's responsibilities.

sources.author: Manmatha Nath Dutt
sources.description: Provides a detailed account of the afterlife and Chitragupta's role in judging souls.
sources.text: Provides a detailed account of the afterlife and Chitragupta's role in judging souls.

sources.author: William Crooke
